pois.name: Misty Fjords Scenic Cruising Area
pois.notes: 2.3 million acre wilderness with 3,000-foot granite cliffs. Cruise ships transit Behm Canal.

pois.name: Behm Canal
pois.notes: One of the longest and deepest fjords in North America. Main waterway for scenic cruising.

pois.name: Rudyerd Bay
pois.notes: Most popular viewing area. Cliffs crowd close to the ship with cascading waterfalls.

pois.name: Punchbowl Cove
pois.notes: Near-circular cove with mirror-still water and dramatic cliff reflections.

pois.name: New Eddystone Rock
pois.notes: Volcanic plug rising 237 feet from the water. Most photographed feature of the monument.

pois.name: Walker Cove
pois.notes: Narrow inlet with towering cliff walls and waterfalls. Less visited than Rudyerd Bay.

pois.name: Nooya Lake
pois.notes: Remote alpine lake accessible by floatplane. Common landing site for aerial tours.

pois.name: Temperate Rainforest
pois.notes: Dense Sitka spruce and western hemlock forest. One of the most intact temperate rainforests on Earth.

pois.name: Winstanley Island
pois.notes: Island at the mouth of Behm Canal. Wildlife viewing and sea bird colonies.

pois.name: Wildlife Viewing Areas
pois.notes: Mountain goats on cliff faces, bald eagles, harbor seals, and black bears along shorelines.
